questions.q: The President of India is the constitutional:
questions.explanation: The President is the constitutional head of state and the first citizen of India.

questions.q: The President of India is described as the country's:
questions.explanation: The President is regarded as the first citizen of India.

questions.q: The President serves as the supreme commander of which institution?
questions.explanation: The President is the supreme commander of the defence forces of India.

questions.q: What is the term of office of the President of India?
questions.explanation: The President holds office for a term of five years.

questions.q: Who was the first President of India?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first President of India, serving from 1950 to 1962.

questions.q: Who was the first woman President of India?
questions.explanation: Pratibha Patil was the first woman President of India, serving from 2007 to 2012.

questions.q: The official residence of the President of India is:
questions.explanation: Rashtrapati Bhavan in New Delhi is the official residence of the President.

questions.q: A bill passed by both Houses of Parliament becomes law after receiving whose assent?
questions.explanation: A bill becomes an Act after it receives the assent of the President.

questions.q: Who appoints the Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: The President appoints the Prime Minister, who is usually the leader of the majority in the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Droupadi Murmu, who assumed office in 2022, is the President with which distinction?
options: First President from a tribal community
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu is the first person from a tribal community and the second woman to become President.

questions.q: The President is elected by an electoral college consisting of elected members of Parliament and:
options: Elected members of state legislative assemblies
questions.explanation: The electoral college comprises elected members of both Houses of Parliament and of the state legislative assemblies.

questions.q: The executive power of the Union is vested in the President under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 53 vests the executive power of the Union in the President.

questions.q: What is the minimum age to be eligible for election as President of India?
questions.explanation: A candidate for President must have completed 35 years of age.

questions.q: The President is elected by which method of voting?
options: Proportional representation by single transferable vote
questions.explanation: The President is elected by proportional representation through the single transferable vote by secret ballot.

questions.q: Which Article states that there shall be a President of India?
questions.explanation: Article 52 provides that there shall be a President of India.

questions.q: The President exercises functions on the aid and advice of which body?
questions.explanation: Under Article 74, the President acts on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers headed by the Prime Minister.

questions.q: The power to grant pardons, reprieves, and remissions of punishment is given to the President under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 72 grants the President the power to pardon, reprieve, respite, or remit punishment.

questions.q: Who administers the oath of office to the President of India?
questions.explanation: The Chief Justice of India, or in their absence the senior-most judge of the Supreme Court, administers the oath to the President.

questions.q: In the event of a vacancy, who acts as President of India?
questions.explanation: The Vice-President discharges the functions of President during a vacancy in that office.

questions.q: Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam, who served as President from 2002 to 2007, was renowned in which field?
questions.explanation: A.P.J. Abdul Kalam was a renowned aerospace scientist before becoming the eleventh President.

questions.q: The President can be removed from office by a process of:
options: Impeachment for violation of the Constitution
questions.explanation: The President can be removed only through impeachment for violation of the Constitution.

questions.q: The procedure for impeachment of the President is laid down in which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 61 lays down the procedure for the impeachment of the President.

questions.q: An impeachment resolution against the President must be passed by each House by a majority of:
options: A simple majority of those present
Three-fourths of those present and voting
Two-thirds of the total membership of that House
An absolute majority of Parliament jointly
questions.explanation: Under Article 61, each House must pass the resolution by a majority of not less than two-thirds of the total membership of that House.

questions.q: The President's power to promulgate ordinances is contained in which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 123 empowers the President to promulgate ordinances when Parliament is not in session.

questions.q: The President may proclaim a national Emergency on grounds of war, external aggression, or armed rebellion under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 352 empowers the President to proclaim a national Emergency on these grounds.

questions.q: If a presidential election dispute arises, it is decided by:
questions.explanation: Disputes relating to the election of the President are inquired into and decided by the Supreme Court.

questions.q: When a bill is presented to the President, the President may return it for reconsideration, except in the case of a:
questions.explanation: Under Article 111, the President may return a bill for reconsideration but cannot do so with a money bill.

questions.q: The President may seek the advisory opinion of the Supreme Court on a question of law under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 143 allows the President to refer a question of law or fact to the Supreme Court for its advisory opinion.

questions.q: Dr. Rajendra Prasad, the first President, holds the distinction of:
options: Having the longest tenure in the office
Being the only President elected unopposed
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad served from 1950 to 1962, the longest tenure of any President of India.

questions.q: A Proclamation of national Emergency under Article 352 must be approved by Parliament within:
questions.explanation: A Proclamation of Emergency under Article 352 must be approved by both Houses within one month to remain in force.
